method_atom: a statistical theory of how quenched spatial disorder in a system with a direction-dependent (anisotropic) ordering interaction produces local fluctuations of the onset parameter for collective order, so that spatially localized ordered domains form and persist above the mean-field ordering threshold; one derives the probability distribution of the local critical parameters and of the local order-parameter amplitude, yielding a heterogeneous medium described as a percolating network of ordered islands embedded in a disordered background whose accessible states are strongly suppressed by residual order-parameter fluctuations
object_atom: a disordered BCS superconductor with unconventional (anisotropic-gap) pairing symmetry: its local pairing temperature, superconducting gap, and superconducting islands above the mean-field transition, with a suppressed electronic density of states, connected to the pseudogap phase and STM-observed gap inhomogeneities of high-temperature superconductors
brief_reason: the disorder-driven local-onset fluctuation and island-formation-above-mean-field machinery transfers across fields, while the Cooper-pairing gap, pairing temperature, and pseudogap/high-Tc physics are the condensed-matter-specific object.
